name: Leak Witch
description: The Leak Witch, or Leyak, is a terrifying creature from Balinese mythology, primarily associated with black magic and shapeshifting. They are believed to be humans who practice dark arts and can transform into various animal forms, most commonly a floating head with entrails hanging below. During the day, they appear as ordinary people, but at night, they detach their heads from their bodies and fly through the air, seeking to consume the blood of pregnant women and newborns. The Leak's power is fueled by their knowledge of ancient spells and rituals, allowing them to manipulate the natural world and inflict illness. They are feared throughout Bali and are often blamed for unexplained deaths and misfortunes. Protection against them includes using holy objects, reciting prayers, and avoiding areas known to be their haunts. The Leak represents the dark side of Balinese magic and the dangers of forbidden knowledge. They are a constant reminder of the importance of maintaining balance between good and evil forces.
subtitle: Floating head of Balinese black magic
appearance: During the day, Leak Witches appear as normal people, often elderly women. At night, they transform into a floating head with internal organs, such as the heart, lungs, and intestines, dangling beneath. Their eyes glow with an eerie red light. Some accounts also describe them transforming into animals like pigs, birds, or monkeys.
habitat: Leak Witches are said to inhabit cemeteries, forests, and other secluded areas. They are often found near villages, preying on unsuspecting victims. They are believed to be more active during the full moon.
behavior: Leak Witches are primarily nocturnal creatures, spending their days disguised as ordinary people. At night, they detach their heads and fly around, searching for pregnant women and newborns to consume their blood. They are driven by a hunger for power and a desire to cause harm. They are also known to cast curses and inflict illness on those who cross their path.
weaknesses: Holy objects (e.g., holy water, blessed kris)

keyMyths.title: The Leak and the Pregnant Woman
keyMyths.description: A Leak Witch targets a pregnant woman, attempting to steal her baby's blood. The woman is protected by her family and a Balinese priest who uses holy water and prayers to repel the Leak.

sources.author: Adrian Vickers
sources.description: Provides insights into Balinese culture and the role of magic and mythology.
sources.text: Provides insights into Balinese culture and the role of magic and mythology.

sources.author: Margaret Mead
sources.description: Offers ethnographic observations on Balinese beliefs and practices, including magic.
